risks: Azure ML Workspace requires an associated Storage Account which is not included in the plan — deployment will fail without it
Application Insights is not included; ML Workspace creates one by default, but omitting it from the plan means no explicit monitoring configuration
Container Registry is not included; required if custom Docker images are used for inference endpoints
No private endpoints configured (usePrivateEndpoints: false) — all resources publicly accessible, which is a security risk for production workloads
No availability zone or geo-redundancy configuration for Cosmos DB — single-region failure could cause data unavailability
No tagging strategy defined for resource governance and cost tracking
No explicit resource cross-references or dependency declarations between ML Workspace and Key Vault
correctionsRecommended: Add an Azure Storage Account resource — this is a mandatory associated resource for Azure ML Workspace
Add an Application Insights resource for ML Workspace monitoring and inference endpoint telemetry
Consider adding an Azure Container Registry if custom inference images will be used
Enable private endpoints (or document why public access is acceptable) to improve network security posture
Add explicit dependency links between resources (ML Workspace → Storage Account, Key Vault, Application Insights)
Configure Cosmos DB backup policy and consider multi-region writes for high-availability scenarios
Add resource tags for cost tracking and governance (e.g., environment, project, owner)
Define an inputs.userGoal field and overallReasoning to document the intended workload and architecture rationale
hardDependencies: Azure ML Workspace requires an associated Storage Account — deployment will fail without this resource in the plan
